root_text.an4.72:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu apaṇṇakappaṭipadaṁ paṭipanno hoti, yoni cassa āraddhā hoti āsavānaṁ khayāya.
root_text.an4.72:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu apaṇṇakappaṭipadaṁ paṭipanno hoti, yoni cassa āraddhā hoti āsavānaṁ khayāyā”ti.

translation_text.an4.72:1.1: “Mendicants, when a mendicant has four things their practice is a sure bet, and they have laid the groundwork for ending the defilements.
translation_text.an4.72:1.3: Thoughts of renunciation, good will, and harmlessness; and right view.
translation_text.an4.72:1.4: When a mendicant possesses these four things their practice is a sure bet, and they have laid the groundwork for ending the defilements.”
